etymology.description: semantic 亻(亻) (rén, "person") + phonetic 壬(壬) (rén, "carry on a shoulder pole") -> to bear duty or responsibility
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 亻(亻) (rén, "person") indicates the concept of an individual, while the phonetic component 壬(壬) (rén, "carry on a shoulder pole") provides the sound. In this case, carrying a load is analogous to bearing responsibility, leading to the meanings 'duty' and 'appoint'.

examples.chinese: 他被任命为经理(他被任命為經理)。
examples.english: He was appointed as the manager.

examples.chinese: 任凭风吹雨打(任憑風吹雨打)，他都坚持上班(他都堅持上班)。
examples.pinyin: Rènpíng fēngchuīyǔdǎ, tā dōu jiānchí shàngbān.
examples.english: Regardless of the wind and rain, he insists on going to work.

examples.chinese: 她在公司中承担了很多责任(她在公司中承擔了很多責任)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài gōngsī zhōng chéngdānle hěn duō zérèn.
examples.english: She has taken on many duties in the company.
